verdict: TIE on primary; eval protocol now valid (C1-C5 addressed in frozen eval_manas.py)

primary flat everywhere (gamma may be too small); rho094 wins best_ood_acc slice 5/5 seeds ~3.5 sigma -> hypothesis, needs held-out; A2 extragradient-vs-memory NOT resolved by primary, best_acc hints LONG memory helps (anti-staleness)

rho094 best_acc FAILED held-out replication (winner's curse; +0.0122 opt -> +0.0013 held-out). All primary deltas < 2 sigma. No promotion. Diagnosis: no positive control exists - testbed sensitivity unknown.

POSITIVE CONTROL PASSES on best_ood_acc (5/5, +0.0136) and train speed; FAILS the frozen frontier metric (nominally negative) -> the primary metric does not carry the Nexus effect on this testbed. Metric change requires user sign-off (frozen eval). Nexus effect here = faster optimization + higher OOD peak.
gamma dose-response roughly monotone on frontier (0.0006/0.0003/0.0016/0.0032 for 1.5e-3/3e-3/5e-3/1e-2); first 2-sigma arm on opt seeds. Held-out pending (wave 3).

PROMOTED: held-out frontier +0.0032 (4 sigma), exact replication of opt-seed delta. Slices clean (best_acc positive, train parity within noise). Champion = gamma 1e-2, rho 0.94, full-d. Note: Manas moves the matched-loss axis; Nexus clone moved the peak-acc axis - different expression, both co-primary going forward (user free-hand sign-off).
dose still rising at gamma 2e-2 (3.2 sigma opt). Champion candidate; held-out queued (wave 4).

DECISIVE: rho=0 at gamma 1e-2 does NOTHING while rho=0.94 gives +0.0032. The multi-batch memory IS the mechanism; pure extragradient contributes zero. Common-minima attribution CONFIRMED at toy scale; A1 staleness empirically weak here. A2 CLOSED.

arm: decomposition: all arms vs base Muon (cached logs, user request)
verdict: Ordering: Muon+Manas > Muon >> AdamW+Nexus > AdamW. Muon dominates AdamW family by ~10x the probe effect; Manas adds a small held-out-replicated increment on top. Caveat: AdamW at Muon lr/wd (C8) - Manas-vs-Muon increment immune (shared hparams).
PROMOTED (2.2 sigma held-out, replicates opt +0.0045; best_acc within noise). Champion: gamma 2e-2, rho 0.94.

arm: exp variants at g10_rho094 (ema/permat/signpos/trust2) + rho098 opt

exp_ema.verdict: REJECTED - standing-slice violation: 2.3x slower training; frontier win covers only high-loss overlap

exp_permat.verdict: tie vs champion; keep as option

exp_signpos.verdict: INERT - effect is descent-direction-specific, not generic smoothing (A10 closed)

exp_trust2.verdict: tie; cap not the lever at toy scale

verdict: INVERSION: the paper-faithful alignment force is injected 10x more cleanly at rho=0 (cos +0.24) than rho=0.94 (+0.02), yet rho=0 is downstream-inert and rho=0.94 wins. Manas gain is NOT Nexus pairwise alignment; hypothesis: trajectory-level lookahead along a variance-reduced (smoothed) descent direction. Quasi-static assumption numerically dead (motion 22-160x ||d||) yet effect survives -> A1 reframed. New candidate: probe along Muon momentum (zero probe memory, purity violated deliberately) - wave 5.
PROMOTED - new champion. Both co-primaries positive on held-out, parity clean. rho window is the strongest axis found.

INERT - momentum-direction probing fails. The active ingredient is the momentum-FREE normalized-gradient EMA with a LONGER window than Muon momentum, distinct from the update direction (Nesterov already probes along momentum inside Muon - redundant). Purity requirement partially rehabilitated. B5 zero-memory shortcut unavailable.
both axes up AND faster training; held-out queued (wave 6)
PROMOTED - champion = gamma 2e-2, rho 0.98 (frontier +0.0088 held-out; best_acc neutral, parity clean).
rho SATURATES at ~0.98 (0.99/0.995 flat vs 0.98). Gamma still rising at 3e-2 and training faster. Dose continues (wave 7: g50, g80 + g30 held-out).
PROMOTED - champion = gamma 3e-2, rho 0.98 (held-out frontier +0.0132, best_acc +0.0097, parity clean; both co-primaries positive).
frontier still rising at 8e-2 and training faster; best_acc component shrinking with gamma (0.0097->0.0042) - possible axis trade opening. Continue dose (wave 8: g80 held-out, g120/g160 opt).
PROMOTED - champion = gamma 8e-2, rho 0.98. Strongest result of the round: held-out frontier +0.0245 (5+ sigma), best_acc positive, trains faster than base.

DOSE CURVE TURNS after ~1.2e-1 (g160 < g120). Optimum region gamma 0.08-0.12 at rho 0.98. Gamma axis CLOSED. Shipped defaults (1e-3/0.85) were ~80x too timid on the toy. A4 resolved empirically: normalized-scale probe works, the knob just needed a dose search.

arm: low-rank at champion config: r32/refresh16, r8/refresh16, r8/refresh200
verdict: rank-8 preserves the full champion effect at ~3 percent probe memory (B1 downstream-impact REFUTED; B5 footprint pitch restored). B2 refresh-mistuning hypothesis REFUTED by its own toggle test: refresh 200 >= refresh 16. Held-out gate for r8/refresh200 queued.

PROMOTED - FINAL CHAMPION: gamma 8e-2, rho 0.98, rank 8, refresh 200. Held-out frontier +0.0255 at ~3 percent probe memory. STOP CONDITION MET (target: held-out > 2 sigma, slices clean, mechanism attributed).

arm: C14 rho x batch-size coupling (bs_rho.py, BS {32,512} x rho {.90,.98,.995})
verdict: USER HYPOTHESIS CONFIRMED: rho optimum falls as batch grows (monotone-up in rho at BS32; rho .90 fully effective at BS512). Invariant: window x BS ~= 5-6k samples across BS 32/128/512. rho reparameterized as memory-in-samples in the manas.py docstring.

arm: comp (user update-history idea) kappa {-1, -0.5, +1} at champion config
verdict: frontier axis unchanged (all == champion). comp+1 adds best_acc +0.0128 (5/5 seeds) - largest Manas peak-acc showing; would make the first both-axes-positive arm. Winning sign is EXTEND along realized travel, not back-off. Held-out gate queued (memento: rho094 best_acc 5/5 died at this gate in wave 1).

NOT PROMOTED: frontier replicates (+0.0266, best nominal number of round but within joint noise of r8 champion +0.0255) while the peak-acc 5/5 signal DIED at held-out (second best_acc winner's-curse this round). comp costs a second full-weights fp32 buffer -> cost-adjusted tie loses to r8 champion. kappa dose turns at +1. Archived as slice-tie; revisit at LM scale where the u buffer could be low-ranked too.

arm: C8 tuned-AdamW baseline (lr {5e-4..8e-3} x wd {.01,.1}, opt seeds)
verdict: C8 CLOSED: tuned optimum interior to grid; EVERY AdamW config loses to plain Muon on every axis at this scale. Muon >> AdamW ordering is not a tuning artifact (toy-scale caveat: orthogonalization-friendly task).

arm: exp_lrdu: low-rank d + low-rank u (shared r8 basis) at champion config, opt + held-out
verdict: All-low-rank VALIDATED: u survives rank-8 projection through d's shared basis (held-out +0.0235, joint-noise tie with r8-no-u +0.0255 and full-u +0.0266). u still does not SEPARATE from no-u at toy scale (batches too homogeneous - user's own prediction). r8-no-u stays champion for simplicity; low-rank u is carry-on-validated for the LM test at ~zero marginal memory.

arm: PSO agreement-weighted votes (vote=agree) vs full-d champion, opt seeds
verdict: TIE (pre-registered criterion: needs >noise held-out to matter). Agreement-weighting the votes neither beats nor breaks the equal-vote average; the 1.4x-gamma control rules out an effective-step confound. PSO social-reinforcement is a good EXPLANATORY LENS for Manas, not a better optimizer at toy scale. Not promoted; no held-out spend. The untested larger door is the gbest ATTRACTOR (vote gating is the inertia term; attraction-to-best is the missing PSO ingredient) - deferred, user to steer.

arm: Shampoo (grafted, Kronecker L^-1/4 M R^-1/4) lr sweep + held-out (C17, friend ablation)
impl_notes: eigh needed ridge-escalation robustness + norm-grafting onto momentum to train stably (Muon/Manas need neither)
verdict: Shampoo joins the non-Muon family: stronger per-step (faster training, min_train well below Muon; small OOD-peak edge +0.009 held-out) but LOSES matched-loss generalization to Muon (-0.0133). Same signature as Adam/Nexus - real curvature helps optimization, not the same-loss/better-model axis. Memory ~3-3.5x Muon (L,R factors + inverse-roots) + eigh compute. Manas r8 (+0.0255) remains the only arm positive on the frontier axis.
